name: Law Offices of Michael A. Latzes
description_short: Philadelphia-based bankruptcy law firm with 40 years of experience offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, foreclosure defense, and family law services with flexible payment plans.
description_long:
Law Offices of Michael A. Latzes is an established bankruptcy and general practice law firm located in Philadelphia, PA, serving Philadelphia and surrounding counties including Montgomery, Bucks, and Delaware. The firm has been operating for 40 years and is a member of the Pennsylvania Bar Association and the National Association of Consumer Bankruptcy Attorneys. Michael A. Latzes is the principal attorney, supported by office staff including an assistant named Eileen based on client testimonials.

The firm offers a comprehensive range of legal services beyond bankruptcy, including Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings, foreclosure defense and law, debt law, family law, divorce proceedings, child custody and support matters, estate planning, wills, and trusts. They emphasize accessibility through multiple service delivery methods: in-person consultations at their Jenkintown office, telephone consultations, and video conferencing via Zoom or FaceTime. Free initial consultations are available to prospective clients.

The firm distinguishes itself through flexible engagement practices: structured payment plans to accommodate clients in financial distress, evening and weekend appointment availability, 24/7 contact availability via phone, text, or email, and a commitment to respond to online questionnaires within 24 hours. Client testimonials consistently highlight the attorney's direct communication style, empathy during financial hardship, and personal attention to case details. The firm explicitly states its goal is to restore clients' financial stability.

While the website demonstrates strong client satisfaction and establishes legitimate credentials, potential clients should note that bankruptcy representation requires careful evaluation of whether Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 is appropriate for their situation. The firm's broad practice areas (including family law and personal injury) suggest a general practice rather than bankruptcy specialization, though bankruptcy appears to be a primary focus based on their website emphasis and bar association membership.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 40 years of legal experience in bankruptcy and related practice areas
Member of Pennsylvania Bar Association and National Association of Consumer Bankruptcy Attorneys
Structured payment plans available for clients in financial distress
Free initial consultations offered to all prospective clients
Multiple service delivery options: in-person, phone, and video conferencing (Zoom, FaceTime)
Evening and weekend appointment availability plus 24/7 contact access
Consistent 5-star client reviews highlighting empathy and professional results
Commitment to respond to online inquiries within 24 hours
cons: Appears to be a general practice firm rather than bankruptcy-specialized, which may limit depth in complex bankruptcy scenarios
Geographic service area limited to Philadelphia and surrounding counties, not suitable for out-of-state clients
Website does not provide pricing information, fee structures, or specific payment plan terms for transparency
No information about success rates, discharge rates, or specific case outcomes to evaluate actual bankruptcy results
Broad practice areas (personal injury, family law, estate law) may indicate divided focus rather than bankruptcy expertise concentration
best_for: Philadelphia-area residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy who need flexible payment arrangements
Homeowners in foreclosure seeking to defend against foreclosure or restructure mortgage debt
Individuals with overwhelming debt seeking debt relief through legal bankruptcy filing
Clients who require flexible scheduling, evening/weekend appointments, or remote consultation options
diagnosis: Law Offices of Michael A. Latzes is best for Philadelphia-area residents facing bankruptcy or foreclosure who value flexible scheduling, affordable payment plans, and personalized attention from an experienced attorney. Primary caveat: this appears to be a general practice firm rather than a bankruptcy-specialized firm, so complex or unusual bankruptcy cases may benefit from consultation with a firm focused exclusively on bankruptcy law.
services: Debt law and debt management counsel
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
